title: tragacantha lanuginosa
intro: Tragacantha lanuginosa, also known as the "woolly goat's-thorn", is a plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its woody stems and white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. Tragacantha lanuginosa has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and wounds.
